                                                                               ZB9.4
STAFF REPORT

To:            Board of Management

From:          Director, Facilities & Infrastructure

Subject:       POLAR BEAR EXHIBIT MODIFICATIONS FOR WALRUS

Date:          2020-05-06


SUMMARY:

This report recommends the award of the tender for the Polar Bear Exhibit Modifications
For Walrus project. The project will upgrade the Life Support systems for the habitat and
provide suitable holding and exhibit space for walrus.

RECOMMENDATIONS:

It is recommended that:

      1. the tender for the Polar Bear Exhibit Modifications for Walrus project be
         awarded to Midome Construction Services Ltd. in the amount of $638,000
         (including 10% owner’s contingency) + HST.

FINANCIAL IMPACT:

There are no changes to the approved 2020 Capital Budget as a result of this
recommendation, as the required funds are allocated within the approved 2020 Capital
Budget under Exhibit Refurbishment.

DECISION HISTORY:

City Council approved the 2020 Capital Budget for the Toronto Zoo at its meeting on
2020-02-19 as per the attached link:

http://app.toronto.ca/tmmis/viewAgendaItemHistory.do?item=2020.EX13.2

COMMENTS:

In late 2019 the Toronto Zoo signed a Loan Agreement with Quebec Aquarium to
exchange polar bears for walruses for a period of 3 years, with the potential for extension.
Walrus are listed as a vulnerable species by the IUCN and wild populations face
significant challenges due to climate change. The Toronto Zoo will participate in research
programs with these animals in collaboration with other accredited facilities. Our
organization has never hosted walrus and this will be a new and exciting experience for
the Zoo and our guests.


Polar Bear Exhibit                                                                Page 1 of 2
The existing polar bear exhibit in the Tundra Trek was renovated in 2009 as part of the
North Zoo Site Redevelopment project. The existing space has three exhibits – one of
which has a large fresh water pool.

This project includes updates to the Life Support systems, modifications to the polar bear
house and changes to two of the exhibits to accommodate walruses. Once construction
is complete, walruses and polar bears will both be able to use the exhibits and would
alternate the use of the spaces. When the Loan Agreement expires the exhibit and
holding space can revert to polar bear occupancy only.

A request for tender (RFT) for construction services was advertised on the Toronto Zoo
website. A project site briefing was held on 2020-01-31. Two (2) submissions were
received on 2020-02-19 as per Schedule I attached. The submissions included a base
bid plus several additional items. The Zoo has elected to proceed with the base bid as
well as items C1 (all work required to enlarge doors D01, D02, D03 & D04 in the polar
bear holding building) and D1 (all work required to enlarge the pool in the maternity
exhibit). All work includes all associated services and rockwork. Based on this, Midome
is the lowest bidder at $580,000 vs. Rutherford’s bid of $676,695.63.

Funding for this project was approved by Council in the 2020 Capital Budget. At this time,
we are requesting approval to engage Midome Construction Services Ltd. for the project.

CONTACTS:

Leona Mitchell, Director
Facilities & Infrastructure
lmitchell@torontozoo.ca
416-392-5985




L. Mitchell
Director, Facilities & Infrastructure

ATTACHMENTS:
Schedule I – Tender Summary – TZC T 03-2020-01
